root_text.sn46.52:1.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pavisiṁsu.
root_text.sn46.52:1.4: Yannūna mayaṁ yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ ārāmo tenupasaṅkameyyāmā”ti.
root_text.sn46.52:2.1: Atha kho te bhikkhū yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ ārāmo tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā tehi aññatitthiyehi paribbājakehi saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
root_text.sn46.52:2.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinne kho te bhikkhū aññatitthiyā paribbājakā etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn46.52:3.1: “Samaṇo, āvuso, gotamo sāvakānaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.sn46.52:3.2: ‘etha tumhe, bhikkhave, pañca nīvaraṇe pahāya cetaso upakkilese paññāya dubbalīkaraṇe satta bojjhaṅge yathābhūtaṁ bhāvethā’ti.
root_text.sn46.52:3.3: Mayampi kho, āvuso, sāvakānaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ desema:
root_text.sn46.52:3.4: ‘etha tumhe, āvuso, pañca nīvaraṇe pahāya cetaso upakkilese paññāya dubbalīkaraṇe satta bojjhaṅge yathābhūtaṁ bhāvethā’ti.
root_text.sn46.52:3.5: Idha no, āvuso, ko viseso, ko adhippayāso, kiṁ nānākaraṇaṁ samaṇassa vā gotamassa amhākaṁ vā, yadidaṁ—dhammadesanāya vā dhammadesanaṁ, anusāsaniyā vā anusāsanin”ti?
root_text.sn46.52:4.1: Atha kho te bhikkhū tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ bhāsitaṁ neva abhinandiṁsu nappaṭikkosiṁsu;
root_text.sn46.52:4.4: Atha kho te bhikkhū sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn46.52:5.1: “Idha mayaṁ, bhante, pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pavisimha.
root_text.sn46.52:5.4: yannūna mayaṁ yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ ārāmo tenupasaṅkameyyāmā’ti.
root_text.sn46.52:5.5: Atha kho mayaṁ, bhante, yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ ārāmo tenupasaṅkamimha; upasaṅkamitvā tehi aññatitthiyehi paribbājakehi saddhiṁ sammodimha.
root_text.sn46.52:5.6: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdimha. Ekamantaṁ nisinne kho amhe, bhante, aññatitthiyā paribbājakā etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn46.52:6.1: ‘Samaṇo, āvuso, gotamo sāvakānaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ deseti “etha tumhe, bhikkhave, pañca nīvaraṇe pahāya cetaso upakkilese paññāya dubbalīkaraṇe satta bojjhaṅge yathābhūtaṁ bhāvethā”ti.
root_text.sn46.52:6.2: Mayampi kho, āvuso, sāvakānaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ desema:
root_text.sn46.52:6.3: “etha tumhe, āvuso, pañca nīvaraṇe pahāya cetaso upakkilese paññāya dubbalīkaraṇe satta bojjhaṅge yathābhūtaṁ bhāvethā”ti.
root_text.sn46.52:6.4: Idha no, āvuso, ko viseso, ko adhippayāso, kiṁ nānākaraṇaṁ samaṇassa vā gotamassa amhākaṁ vā, yadidaṁ—dhammadesanāya vā dhammadesanaṁ, anusāsaniyā vā anusāsanin’ti?
root_text.sn46.52:7.1: Atha kho mayaṁ, bhante, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ bhāsitaṁ neva abhinandimha nappaṭikkosimha, anabhinanditvā appaṭikkositvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkamimha:
root_text.sn46.52:8.2: ‘atthi panāvuso, pariyāyo, yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma pañca nīvaraṇā dasa honti, satta bojjhaṅgā catuddasā’ti.
root_text.sn46.52:8.3: Evaṁ puṭṭhā, bhikkhave, aññatitthiyā paribbājakā na ceva sampāyissanti, uttariñca vighātaṁ āpajjissanti.
root_text.sn46.52:8.6: Nāhaṁ taṁ, bhikkhave, passāmi sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya, yo imesaṁ pañhānaṁ veyyākaraṇena cittaṁ ārādheyya, aññatra tathāgatena vā tathāgatasāvakena vā ito vā pana sutvā.
root_text.sn46.52:9.1: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, pariyāyo, yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma pañca nīvaraṇā dasa honti?
root_text.sn46.52:9.2: Yadapi, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ kāmacchando tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ, yadapi bahiddhā kāmacchando tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn46.52:9.3: ‘Kāmacchandanīvaraṇan’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.52:9.4: Yadapi, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ byāpādo tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ, yadapi bahiddhā byāpādo tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn46.52:9.5: ‘Byāpādanīvaraṇan’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.52:9.6: Yadapi, bhikkhave, thinaṁ tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ, yadapi middhaṁ tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn46.52:9.7: ‘Thinamiddhanīvaraṇan’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.52:9.8: Yadapi, bhikkhave, uddhaccaṁ tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ, yadapi kukkuccaṁ tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn46.52:9.9: ‘Uddhaccakukkuccanīvaraṇan’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.52:9.10: Yadapi, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ dhammesu vicikicchā tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ, yadapi bahiddhā dhammesu vicikicchā tadapi nīvaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn46.52:9.11: ‘Vicikicchānīvaraṇan’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.52:9.12: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pariyāyo, yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma pañca nīvaraṇā dasa honti.
root_text.sn46.52:10.1: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, pariyāyo, yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma satta bojjhaṅgā catuddasa honti?
root_text.sn46.52:10.2: Yadapi, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ dhammesu sati tadapi satisambojjhaṅgo, yadapi bahiddhā dhammesu sati tadapi satisambojjhaṅgo.
root_text.sn46.52:10.3: ‘Satisambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.52:11.1: Yadapi, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ dhammesu paññāya pavicinati pavicarati parivīmaṁsamāpajjati tadapi dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo, yadapi bahiddhā dhammesu paññāya pavicinati pavicarati parivīmaṁsamāpajjati tadapi dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo.
root_text.sn46.52:11.2: ‘Dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.52:12.1: Yadapi, bhikkhave, kāyikaṁ vīriyaṁ tadapi vīriyasambojjhaṅgo, yadapi cetasikaṁ vīriyaṁ tadapi vīriyasambojjhaṅgo.
root_text.sn46.52:12.2: ‘Vīriyasambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.52:13.1: Yadapi, bhikkhave, savitakkasavicārā pīti tadapi pītisambojjhaṅgo, yadapi avitakkaavicārā pīti tadapi pītisambojjhaṅgo.
root_text.sn46.52:13.2: ‘Pītisambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.52:14.1: Yadapi, bhikkhave, kāyappassaddhi tadapi passaddhisambojjhaṅgo, yadapi cittappassaddhi tadapi passaddhisambojjhaṅgo.
root_text.sn46.52:14.2: ‘Passaddhisambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.52:15.1: Yadapi, bhikkhave, savitakko savicāro samādhi tadapi samādhisambojjhaṅgo, yadapi avitakkaavicāro samādhi tadapi samādhisambojjhaṅgo.
root_text.sn46.52:15.2: ‘Samādhisambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.52:16.1: Yadapi, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ dhammesu upekkhā tadapi upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo, yadapi bahiddhā dhammesu upekkhā tadapi upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo.
root_text.sn46.52:16.2: ‘Upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo’ti iti hidaṁ uddesaṁ gacchati. Tadamināpetaṁ pariyāyena dvayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.52:16.3: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pariyāyo, yaṁ pariyāyaṁ āgamma satta bojjhaṅgā catuddasā”ti.

translation_text.sn46.52:1.1: Then several mendicants robed up in the morning and, taking their bowls and robes, entered Sāvatthī for alms.
translation_text.sn46.52:1.3: “It’s too early to wander for alms in Sāvatthī.
translation_text.sn46.52:1.4: Why don’t we visit the monastery of the wanderers of other religions?”
translation_text.sn46.52:2.1: Then they went to the monastery of the wanderers of other religions and exchanged greetings with the wanderers there.
translation_text.sn46.52:2.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they sat down to one side. The wanderers said to them:
translation_text.sn46.52:3.1: “Reverends, the ascetic Gotama teaches his disciples like this:
translation_text.sn46.52:3.2: ‘Mendicants, please give up the five hindrances—corruptions of the heart that weaken wisdom—and truly develop the seven awakening factors.’
translation_text.sn46.52:3.4: ‘Reverends, please give up the five hindrances—corruptions of the heart that weaken wisdom—and truly develop the seven awakening factors.’
translation_text.sn46.52:3.5: What, then, is the difference between the ascetic Gotama’s teaching and instruction and ours?”
translation_text.sn46.52:4.1: Those mendicants neither approved nor rejected that statement of the wanderers of other religions.
translation_text.sn46.52:4.2: They got up from their seat, thinking:
translation_text.sn46.52:4.3: “We will learn the meaning of this statement from the Buddha himself.”
translation_text.sn46.52:4.4: Then, after the meal, when they returned from almsround, they went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened.
translation_text.sn46.52:8.1: “Mendicants, when wanderers of other religions say this, you should say to them:
translation_text.sn46.52:8.2: ‘But reverends, is there a way in which the five hindrances become ten and the seven awakening factors become fourteen?’
translation_text.sn46.52:8.3: Questioned like this, the wanderers of other religions would be stumped, and, in addition, would get frustrated.
translation_text.sn46.52:8.5: Because they’re out of their element.
translation_text.sn46.52:8.6: I don’t see anyone in this world—with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans—who could provide a satisfying answer to these questions except for the Realized One or his disciple or someone who has heard it from them.
translation_text.sn46.52:9.1: And what is the way in which the five hindrances become ten?
translation_text.sn46.52:9.2: Sensual desire for what is internal is a hindrance; and sensual desire for what is external is also a hindrance.
translation_text.sn46.52:9.3: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the hindrance of sensual desire’ becomes twofold.
translation_text.sn46.52:9.4: Ill will for what is internal is a hindrance; and ill will for what is external is also a hindrance.
translation_text.sn46.52:9.5: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the hindrance of ill will’ becomes twofold.
translation_text.sn46.52:9.6: Dullness is a hindrance; and drowsiness is also a hindrance.
translation_text.sn46.52:9.7: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the hindrance of dullness and drowsiness’ becomes twofold.
translation_text.sn46.52:9.8: Restlessness is a hindrance; and remorse is also a hindrance.
translation_text.sn46.52:9.9: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the hindrance of restlessness and remorse’ becomes twofold.
translation_text.sn46.52:9.10: Doubt about internal things is a hindrance; and doubt about external things is also a hindrance.
translation_text.sn46.52:9.11: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the hindrance of doubt’ becomes twofold.
translation_text.sn46.52:9.12: This is the way in which the five hindrances become ten.
translation_text.sn46.52:10.1: And what is the way in which the seven awakening factors become fourteen?
translation_text.sn46.52:10.2: Mindfulness of internal things is the awakening factor of mindfulness; and mindfulness of external things is also the awakening factor of mindfulness.
translation_text.sn46.52:10.3: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of mindfulness’ becomes twofold.
translation_text.sn46.52:11.1: Investigating, exploring, and inquiring into internal things with wisdom is the awakening factor of investigation of principles; and investigating, exploring, and inquiring into external things with wisdom is also the awakening factor of investigation of principles.
translation_text.sn46.52:11.2: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of investigation of principles’ becomes twofold.
translation_text.sn46.52:12.1: Physical energy is the awakening factor of energy; and mental energy is also the awakening factor of energy.
translation_text.sn46.52:12.2: That’s how what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of energy’ becomes twofold.
translation_text.sn46.52:13.1: Rapture while placing the mind and keeping it connected is the awakening factor of rapture; and rapture without placing the mind and keeping it connected is also the awakening factor of rapture.
translation_text.sn46.52:13.2: In this way what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of rapture’ becomes twofold.
translation_text.sn46.52:14.1: Physical tranquility is the awakening factor of tranquility; and mental tranquility is also the awakening factor of tranquility.
translation_text.sn46.52:14.2: In this way what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of tranquility’ becomes twofold.
translation_text.sn46.52:15.1: Immersion while placing the mind and keeping it connected is the awakening factor of immersion; and immersion without placing the mind and keeping it connected is also the awakening factor of immersion.
translation_text.sn46.52:15.2: In this way what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of immersion’ becomes twofold.
translation_text.sn46.52:16.1: Equanimity for internal things is the awakening factor of equanimity; and equanimity for external things is also the awakening factor of equanimity.
translation_text.sn46.52:16.2: In this way what is concisely referred to as ‘the awakening factor of equanimity’ becomes twofold.
translation_text.sn46.52:16.3: This is the way in which the seven awakening factors become fourteen.”
